faqs.q: Is hopelessness a symptom of depression?
faqs.a: Yes, it's a common and well-recognized symptom of depression, and it can also accompany grief, chronic illness, and prolonged stress. Because it's a symptom, it can be treated, even though it can feel permanent from the inside.

faqs.q: What can I do if I feel hopeless right now?
faqs.a: Please know you don't have to carry this alone, and support is available right now. In the US you can call or text 988, the Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, any time, day or night, to talk with someone trained to help.

faqs.q: Does feeling hopeless mean something is wrong with me?
faqs.a: No, hopelessness isn't a character flaw or a sign of weakness, and caring, capable, strong people can still feel it when they're unwell. Naming it as a symptom is the first step toward getting it to lift, and it's very treatable.
